name: La Selva MTB Route 11: El Soler
name_original: Espai Natural de Les Guilleries – circular desde Sant Hilari Sacalm
description: Explore the moderate 15.1-mile La Selva MTB Route 11: El Soler through the forested Les Guilleries, gaining 2695 feet.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Sant Hilari Sacalm are set within the Guilleries mountains, a region known for its dense forests, numerous natural springs, and varied terrain. The landscape features flowing streams, dramatic gorges, and a mix of singletrack, doubletrack, and dirt roads. This area provides a diverse environment for mountain biking, ranging from challenging climbs to exhilarating descents. Best mountain bike trails around Sant Hilari Sacalm The most popular mountain bike trail is La Selva MTB Route 11: El Soler, a moderate 15.1 miles (24.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 31 minutes to complete. This route offers a mix of terrain through the region's forests. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Santa Margarida de Vallors – Ermita de El Padró loop from Sant Hilari Sacalm, a moderate 15.4 miles (24.7 km) path. This route explores forest landscapes and passes by the Ermita de El Padró. Local mountain bikers also love the Cabanes als Arbres Treehouses – Turó de la Guàrdia loop from Sant Hilari Sacalm, a 24.2 miles (39.0 km) trail leading through the Guilleries mountains, often completed in about 3 hours 31 minutes. Mountain biking around Sant Hilari Sacalm is defined by dense forests, mountainous terrain, and flowing streams. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Sant Hilari Sacalm are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 70 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Sant Hilari Sacalm's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Sant Hilari Sacalm?
A: The mountain bike trails around Sant Hilari Sacalm offer a diverse range of terrain. You'll find yourself riding through dense forests, along flowing streams, and navigating dramatic gorges within the Guilleries mountains. The network includes singletrack, doubletrack, dirt roads, and even some paved sections, providing a varied experience for all skill levels.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Sant Hilari Sacalm?
A: There are over 50 mountain bike trails around Sant Hilari Sacalm listed on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, with 1 easy, 23 moderate, and 26 difficult options to explore.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Sant Hilari Sacalm are designed as loops. For example, you could try the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31466794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Margarida de Vallors – Ermita de El Padró loop from Sant Hilari Sacalm&lt;/a&gt;, which explores forest landscapes and passes by the Ermita de El Padró. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3984095" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabanes als Arbres Treehouses – Turó de la Guàrdia loop from Sant Hilari Sacalm&lt;/a&gt;, a longer moderate route through the Guilleries mountains.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Sant Hilari Sacalm?
A: The mountain bike trails around Sant Hilari Sacalm are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. Many riders praise the diverse terrain, the beauty of the dense forests, and the challenging yet rewarding climbs and descents found in the Guilleries mountains.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Sant Hilari Sacalm offers 26 difficult mountain bike routes for experienced riders. A challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/11790213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabanes als Arbres Treehouses – Osor Village loop from Sant Hilari Sacalm&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 58 km with significant elevation gain. Another demanding route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/11787358" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Farners Castle – Santa Coloma Lookout Rock loop from Sant Hilari Sacalm&lt;/a&gt;, spanning over 53 km.

Q: What kind of natural features can I see while mountain biking in Sant Hilari Sacalm?
A: Sant Hilari Sacalm, known as 'the town of 100 springs,' is rich in natural beauty. While mountain biking, you'll encounter dense forests, flowing streams, and dramatic gorges. The region is also home to picturesque waterfalls, contributing to a verdant and dynamic environment.

Q: Are there any specific mountain bike parks or dedicated trails in the area?
A: Yes, there are several dedicated mountain bike highlights in the vicinity. You can explore trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5782470" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Spiderman Trail&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4839718" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dragon Khan Trail&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5660274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senglanota XXL&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of the local bike park offerings.

Q: Can I find mountain passes or viewpoints along the trails?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain of the Guilleries offers several passes and potential viewpoints. Notable passes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/830269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Condreu&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3219752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bordoriol Pass&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1111103" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Martí Sacalm&lt;/a&gt;. These locations often provide expansive views of the surrounding landscapes.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical points of interest accessible by mountain bike?
A: Yes, the region combines natural beauty with cultural interest. The 'Ruta de les Ermites' (Hermitages Route) is an example of a trail that allows you to explore the forest landscape by bike while encountering historical hermitages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31466794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Margarida de Vallors – Ermita de El Padró loop from Sant Hilari Sacalm&lt;/a&gt; specifically passes by the Ermita de El Padró.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Sant Hilari Sacalm?
A: Given the region's lush, water-rich landscapes and dense forests, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking. The temperatures are milder, and the scenery is particularly vibrant. Summer can be warm, but the forest trails provide good shade. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail available around Sant Hilari Sacalm. The varied terrain also means that some sections of longer routes might be suitable for less experienced riders, but it's always best to check the route details and elevation profiles carefully.

faqs.question: How long does it take to mountain bike La Selva MTB Route 11: El Soler?
faqs.answer: This route typically takes around 2 hours and 30 minutes to complete by mountain bike. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is La Selva MTB Route 11: El Soler suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is rated as moderate. While it offers a good balance for those who enjoy nature, its distance of over 24 km and elevation gain of 821 meters mean it's best suited for riders with some mountain biking experience rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the El Soler MTB route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends near Sant Hilari Sacalm. You can find parking options within Sant Hilari Sacalm, which serves as a convenient base for accessing trails in the Les Guilleries area.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the El Soler MTB route?
faqs.answer: The route winds through the dense, green forests of Les Guilleries. You'll experience varied and typical forest scenery of the region, characterized by rolling, heavily forested mountainous areas.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the way, you'll encounter several points of interest. These include the Pou de Glaç (Ice Well) of the Font Picant spring, the historic house of El Soler, and stretches alongside the El Gavatx stream. You can also enjoy a View of Montseny.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike La Selva MTB Route 11: El Soler?
faqs.answer: The lush forest environment of Les Guilleries makes this route enjoyable during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the foliage is vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, especially in the shaded forest sections, but be mindful of warmer temperatures. Winter rides are possible but check weather conditions for potential rain or colder temperatures.

faqs.question: Is La Selva MTB Route 11: El Soler dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through natural areas and potentially near livestock, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to ensure their safety and respect local wildlife and other trail users.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route in Espai Natural de les Guilleries-Savassona?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trails within the Espai Natural de les Guilleries-Savassona for activities like mountain biking. However, always respect local regulations and signage within the natural park.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several other official trails. Notably, it covers 61% of the BTT la Selva - 11. El Soler route, 33% of the Camí del nord o del Canigó, and 31% of the BTT la Selva - 12. Les Ermites. You'll also find smaller segments overlapping with the BTT la Selva - Circular Nord and the Via cicloturística InterCatalunya.

faqs.question: What amenities are available near the El Soler trail?
faqs.answer: The nearby town of Sant Hilari Sacalm offers various amenities, including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. It's a good place to refuel or relax before or after your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for mountain biking this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and length, bring plenty of water, snacks, a basic repair kit for your bike, and appropriate clothing for the weather. A helmet is essential, and consider a first-aid kit. A navigation device with the route loaded is also highly recommended.
